svg.title: OfferDyne — Scenario → Strategy → Output Matrix
svg.desc: Five scenarios on the same base account (LC-PL-10001, ₹3.12L, DPD_60) showing how different borrower signals produce different strategies and offers, all within guardrails.
svg.text: PS#8 — same outstanding, different borrower signals, different offers. Dynamic negotiation in action.
I just can't pay this amount right now."
"I can pay, just not everything right now.
Can we split it over a couple of months?"
month. Total unchanged, closure in Q3."
was cleared only for today. After 6pm
"I have three loans with HDFC, why
are you only talking about one?"
"I don't owe this — I already paid!
Stop calling or I'll go to court."
"I hear you — let me route this to our
proof. They'll call you in 24 hours."
De-escalate → process exit, not price exit
Why this is PS#8's "dynamic negotiation" in action
Same base account (LC-PL-10001 · ₹3.12L · DPD_60) — yet every row produces a different offer because the borrower signal is different.
The brief's own test sentence — "I lost my job" vs "I can pay, just not everything" — maps to rows 1 vs 2: LOWER vs REFRAME, not the same price.
Row 5 is the judge-attack case: 3 rejections at floor do NOT force a breach — the system picks ESCALATE instead. 100% guardrail compliance proved on the hardest path.
